rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical β-chlorosulfoxidation of alkenes using thiols and HCl as limiting reagents with water as the oxygen source
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of thiophenol generates a thiyl radical; subsequent anodic oxidations convert the β-thioether radical to an episulfonium cation and then oxidize the sulfide to a sulfoxide; cathode reduces protons to H2
electron_flow: Anode oxidizes ArSH to a thiyl radical (in equilibrium with ArSSAr/disulfide radical cation); the thiyl radical adds to the alkene to give a β-thio C-radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to an episulfonium cation that is captured by chloride to give a β-chloro sulfide; a second anodic SET on sulfur followed by water attack and deprotonation delivers the β-chlorosulfoxide, while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: H2 18O isotope labeling
probe_reagents.observation: Sulfoxide O is 95:5 18O/16O, confirming water as the oxygen source

probe_reagents.name: vinylcyclopropane (radical clock, 56)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 57 obtained (10%, 2:1 dr), consistent with a thiyl/carbon-radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: β-hydroxyl sulfoxide 47
probe_reagents.observation: Submission of 47 under standard conditions gave only trace 3, ruling out β-hydroxy sulfoxide as a competent intermediate

probe_reagents.name: sampling/time-course of electrolysis
probe_reagents.observation: Disulfide (Int1), β-functionalized sulfides (Int2–5) and β-hydroxy sulfoxide (Int6) accumulate in the first hour; β-chlorosulfoxide 8 forms only afterward, supporting β-functionalized organosulfur species as on-cycle intermediates

notes: A parallel path b is proposed where the alkene is first oxidized to a radical cation that is then trapped by ArS•/ArSSAr to converge on the episulfonium ion F. The β-hydroxy sulfoxide pathway (via Int6/47) is explicitly excluded by the control experiment, so chloride capture of the episulfonium ion precedes S-oxidation.
